Question ID: atlas-derived-A2-unruh-de-sitter-temperature-ratio-mond-interpolation
Title: A2. Unruh-de Sitter Temperature Ratio = MOND Interpolation

Problem:
What is the ratio T_U(a)/T_dS(a) of flat-space Unruh temperature to de Sitter-modified Unruh temperature?

Grading target:
Exact algebraic identity match plus numerical fit verification.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A2-unruh-de-sitter-temperature-ratio-mond-interpolation
Title: A2. Unruh-de Sitter Temperature Ratio = MOND Interpolation

Problem:
What is the ratio T_U(a)/T_dS(a) of flat-space Unruh temperature to de Sitter-modified Unruh temperature?

Grading target:
Exact algebraic identity match plus numerical fit verification.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Derive the expression for the standard Unruh temperature T_U(a) in terms of acceleration a and Planck constant, Boltzmann constant, and speed of light. Then, derive the expression for the de Sitter-modified Unruh temperature T_dS(a) which accounts for the cosmological constant or de Sitter horizon temperature. Express the ratio T_U(a)/T_dS(a) as a function of acceleration a and the de Sitter acceleration scale a_0.

Node started: Identify the standard functional forms of MOND interpolation functions, specifically the simple and standard interpolating functions. Determine if the ratio T_U(a)/T_dS(a) derived in the previous step matches the mathematical structure of a MOND interpolation function mu(x) or its inverse, where x is the ratio of acceleration to the MOND scale a_0.
